scoring_model.recommendation: Use editable YAML config for weights. Default weights should match the model below, but users should be able to tune them without editing Python code.

component.name: agency_relevance
component.rule: Tier 1 agency = 25; Tier 2 = 15; other DoD = 10; civilian = 5.

component.name: technical_capability_match
component.rule: Score based on keyword matches across title, description, NAICS, PSC, and tags.

component.name: contract_size_fit
component.rule: For solo founder: ideal initial target is small subcontracting, pilot, prototype, BPA call, task order, or niche analytics support. Penalize very large full-and-open efforts unless teaming potential is high.

component.name: teaming_potential
component.rule: Score higher when incumbent or likely prime exists, award is too large for solo prime, and work aligns with niche capability.

component.name: timing_recompete_window
component.rule: Due soon but actionable = high. Expiring contracts within 6-18 months = high. Already expired or due immediately = lower.

scoring_model.example_scoring_weights_yaml: opportunity_score: agency_relevance: 25 technical_capability_match: 25 contract_size_fit: 20 teaming_potential: 20 timing_recompete_window: 10 agency_tiers: tier_1: ["Department of the Navy", "NAVSEA", "Team Submarine", "CDAO", "DISA"] tier_2: ["Air Force", "Army", "DoD"] keywords: high_value: ["data analytics", "Power BI", "AI", "machine learning", "knowledge management", "SharePoint", "RAG", "LLM", "digital transformation", "data governance"] size_fit: ideal_min: 50000 ideal_max: 3000000

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Scores are transparent and explainable in the UI.
User can see score component breakdown.
Weights can be modified in config/scoring_weights.yml.
